items.reason: All 20 decisions (DEC-001 through DEC-020) in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` have id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to. The newer DEC-016 through DEC-020 explicitly cover dictionary generation direction, canonical field reconciliation, global enum crosswalks, closed privacy classes, and migration generation direction as required.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (target 2026-05-15) and PEND-005 (target 2026-05-03), both with owner Andy, blockers, and rationale. Target dates are future relative to research date 2026-04-24/26.

Each decision's consequences section names concrete artifacts removed. E.g. DEC-001 'Eliminates automatic merging of credential subjects', DEC-010 'Removes the need for application code to perform per-table tenant filtering', DEC-016 'Removes five-way hand-edited dictionary reconciliation'. Specific code paths and categories are named.

Options bracket the design space credibly. DEC-005 considers 'AGS as the only canonical gradebook' (aggressive collapse), peer source-native stores (permissive), and the chosen middle. DEC-009 considers QTI-as-canonical-content (real production choice some platforms make). DEC-016 considers shared-only generation, per-spec canonical, and the chosen direction.

DEC-016 declares the shared `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream and per-spec dictionaries should be generated, but there is no `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py`. The committed per-spec dictionaries (`dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json`, `qti-core.v1.json`, `case-core.v1.json`, `caliper-core.v1.json`, `integration-governance-core.v1.json`) are each 30k+ hand-edited and not generated from any shared seed. `check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates per-spec→artifact consistency but never compares to a shared upstream. The decision is documented but unimplemented.

DEC-017 mandates `canonical_field_id` on every overlapping spec field, but no per-spec dictionary field carries this attribute. OneRoster `person.email`, Caliper `caliper_actor.identifier_value`, and LTI `lti_membership.email` each have `sourceStandard` references but none point to a canonical shared field — they're independent records. Cross-spec resolution exists only in DEC-001/DEC-017 prose.

No `canonical_object_id` attribute appears on `person`, `caliper_actor`, `lti_membership`, or QTI candidate-shaped objects in any per-spec dictionary. The shared `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is not visible as containing a canonical identity entity that other specs reference. Unification exists only as DEC-001 prose, which the rubric explicitly fails.

Per-spec dictionaries use `allowed_values_ref` to share enum lists *within* a single per-spec file (e.g., `event_type` in caliper-core), but no `shared_enum_id` attribute references a global cross-spec enum. OneRoster role values, LTI URI roles, Caliper actor types, and QTI item types are declared independently. DEC-018 was made but the data-level crosswalk does not exist.

DEC-019 declares `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` invalid, but `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json` still uses them: `caliper_entity.name` has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_entity"`, `caliper_entity.description` likewise, and `caliper_entity.extensions` has `"privacy_class": "depends_on_contents"`. The decision contradicts the data.

`supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql` is a 19k hand-written DDL with foreign keys, RLS policies, and check constraints. None of the per-spec dictionaries declare `relationships` records that could generate it. No migration generator exists. DEC-020 was made (the rubric requires this) but the dictionary's relational graph that drives migrations does not exist.

Every field in the shown per-spec dictionaries carries `decision_id` (e.g., `DEC-007-identifier-crosswalk`, `DEC-008-time-session`). `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` validates that each `decision_id` matches a known decision and appears in the decision-trace block. Object-level decision citation is implicit through field decisions plus the decision register's `projects_to` list.

Per-spec dictionary HTML/Markdown pages (`docs/generated/*-dictionary.md`, `site/docs/*-dictionary.html`) are generated from the per-spec JSON via `scripts/generate_*_core.py`. However, prose docs like `docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` describe ledger detail in hand-written tables without per-claim links to dictionary anchors, and the rubric requires every load-bearing claim to link to a dictionary anchor. Generated coverage is real but partial.

`site/index.html` renders decision cards parsed from `standards-overlap-decisions.md` with question, choice, consequences, and projects_to per decision. This satisfies 'plain language and link to the decision register' for the cards shown. However, doc pages elsewhere (e.g., `docs/lead-spec-accounting.md`) cite decisions only by ID without explaining the simplification produced for that concept in plain language at the point of use. Partial coverage.

No `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` exists in the evidence. README.md and the portal link to backlog, dictionary, and OpenAPI docs but not to a layperson end-to-end build guide covering create-school → enroll-student → post-grade → link-to-CASE → read-Caliper-feed with copy-pasteable executable examples. The guide is required by the rubric and is absent.

`site/docs/index.html` lists 24 generated/rendered doc pages reachable from the portal. No automated link checker output is shown. Without a buildability guide it cannot be reachable, and CASE/QTI/Caliper/LTI runtime endpoints are documented as planned, so reachability of every dictionary entry from at least one doc cannot be confirmed.

Multiple gaps prevent a layperson developer from completing the fixed test scenario: (1) no buildability guide exists (traces to docs_include_buildability_guide → missing `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md`); (2) creating a school org and enrolling students requires write APIs that don't exist — the hosted runtime is read-only OneRoster plus Edge Function receipts (traces to runtime gap acknowledged in DEC-012); (3) posting a grade is possible via gradebook-bulk-submit but no doc walks through obtaining a tenant-scoped JWT; (4) linking to a CASE standard URI has no runtime endpoint (CASE is doc-only per DEC-012); (5) reading a Caliper-style feed has no read endpoint, only ingestion receipts. Each gap traces to a specific decision (DEC-012) or missing doc (`docs/build-an-edtech-app.md`).

`scripts/evaluate_platform.py` builds verdicts with fields `id, status, reason, evidence_paths, blocked_prerequisites` (see the explicit list in `build_evaluator_prompt` and the verdict assembly loop). The rubric requires a `traced_cause` field on every non-pass item, plus a `buildability_gaps` array. Neither field is constructed, requested in the prompt, or written to `site/api/platform-evaluation.json` by the script.

`VERIFY.md` ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, so the evaluator runs each iteration. The script writes counts, done flag, items, projections, advisory score, and prereq status. However, items lack `traced_cause` and the report lacks `buildability_gaps`, both required by the rubric's substance_check.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `read_overrides()` and `overrides_paused()`, calls them at the start of every iteration before invoking Codex, halts on PAUSE before the next iteration runs, and `build_prompt()` injects overrides under a literal 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE …)' section.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` reads `after_eval.get('done')` and returns 0 when true: 'Evaluator reports `done` after this iteration. Stopping the loop.' Stall detection and max-iteration safety rails apply on top, but termination references the evaluator's `done` flag, not a numeric score threshold.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
